prompts.label: Create Django project
prompts.text:
I'm a beginner with Django. Guide me to create a new project from scratch.

- venv, pip install django, django-admin startproject config .
- Explain settings.py, urls.py, manage.py
- python manage.py runserver and open admin URL path

Use Django 5.x commands. List files created.

prompts.label: First app and hello view
prompts.text:
Add a Django app with one view.

- python manage.py startapp pages
- Register app in INSTALLED_APPS
- View hello returning HttpResponse Hello Django
- Wire urlpatterns in project urls.py

Show every file change.

prompts.label: Render HTML template
prompts.text:
Teach Django templates for a beginner.

- templates/home.html extending base.html with block content
- View uses render(request, 'home.html', context)
- Pass title variable in context
- Configure TEMPLATES DIRS in settings

Provide template syntax for {{ title }} and {% block %}.

prompts.label: Model and makemigrations
prompts.text:
Create a first Django model.

- Model Article: title (CharField max 200), body (TextField), created_at (auto)
- makemigrations and migrate commands explained
- Register in admin.py
- Create superuser steps

Show models.py and admin registration.

prompts.label: Customize Django admin
prompts.text:
Improve Django admin for the Article model.

- list_display, list_filter, search_fields
- readonly_fields for created_at
- Short explanation of admin site for development

Assume Article model exists.

prompts.label: ModelForm create article
prompts.text:
Add a form to create an Article (beginner).

- ModelForm for Article (title, body)
- View + template with {% csrf_token %} and form.as_p
- Redirect to list after success
- URL route /articles/new/

Explain CSRF in one sentence.

prompts.label: ListView and DetailView
prompts.text:
Refactor to class-based views for Article.

- ArticleListView, ArticleDetailView
- Templates article_list.html and article_detail.html
- urls.py with pk in path

Compare CBV vs function view in 3 bullets for beginners.

prompts.label: Static CSS in Django
prompts.text:
Add CSS to a Django project for development.

- static/css/styles.css linked in base.html with {% load static %}
- STATIC_URL and STATICFILES_DIRS in settings
- collectstatic mentioned for production only

Simple example: style the navbar.

prompts.label: DRF hello API endpoint
prompts.text:
Introduce Django REST framework with one endpoint.

- pip install djangorestframework, add to INSTALLED_APPS
- APIView or @api_view GET returning JSON { message: 'hello' }
- Wire url /api/hello/
- Test with browser or curl

Do not build full CRUD yet; explain what DRF is for.

prompts.label: Before deploy checklist
prompts.text:
Give a beginner checklist before deploying Django (conceptual, any host).

- Set DEBUG=False, configure ALLOWED_HOSTS
- Environment variable for SECRET_KEY
- Database not sqlite for production (mention Postgres)
- Static files whitenoise or CDN one line

No full deploy steps — learning checklist only.

prompts.label: reverse() and URL names in templates
prompts.text:
I hard-code href paths. Show url tag with name= and kwargs.
Explain why route renames become safer.
Demonstrate reverse() in tests or views building redirects.
Touch on app namespace patterns lightly.
Include a mini urls.py + path name example.

prompts.label: Middleware request/response flow
prompts.text:
Explain Django middleware as onion layers with process_request and process_response.
Give a toy middleware adding X-Process-Time without external libs.
Highlight order significance in MIDDLEWARE list.
Note async middleware caution briefly for beginners.
Suggest security middlewares as read-the-docs homework.

prompts.label: select_related and prefetch_related
prompts.text:
Teach the N+1 problem with a blog author example.
Show select_related for forward ForeignKey and prefetch_related for reverse many-to-many.
Explain prefetch_related query count intuition.
Demonstrate Prefetch object name-drop for advanced control.
Relate to django-debug-toolbar as a visual aid mention only.

prompts.label: Custom template filter registration
prompts.text:
Build a filter currency_cents that formats integers safely.
Show app ready in apps.py or traditional templatetags package layout.
Explain autoescaping interaction for HTML-returning filters.
Add simple unit test idea using RequestFactory render.
Warn about business logic drowning in filters.

prompts.label: Management command scaffold
prompts.text:
Create a Django management command export_users_csv with options dry-run.
Sketch handle() argparse-style arguments.
Explain stdout.write style messaging.
Show discovery under management/commands/*.py.
Mention transactional behavior when touching DB lightly.

prompts.label: Messages framework flashes
prompts.text:
Wire django.contrib.messages for success alerts after POST-redirect-get.
Show MessageMiddleware requirement and template iteration.
Differentiate levels info/success/error.
Explain cookie vs session backends at a headline level.
Give security note about escaping message content.

prompts.label: File uploads overview
prompts.text:
Outline FileField/ImageField modeling and form enctype multipart.
Describe MEDIA_ROOT/MEDIA_URL split concerns.
Mention validating size/extension at form layer.
Touch on serving files in dev vs prod (whitenoise/S3 conceptual).
List one security pitfall executing user uploads.

prompts.label: LoginRequired mixin on CBVs
prompts.text:
Show LoginRequiredMixin order placement with CBVs.
Compare to @login_required for function views.
Explain redirect LOGIN_URL interplay.
Add UserPassesTestMixin teaser for roles.
Connect to middleware AuthenticationMiddleware.

prompts.label: Testing with Django Client
prompts.text:
Write tests hitting a view with self.client.post including CSRF exempt testing notes.
Use assertRedirects and assertContains.
Explain TransactionTestCase vs TestCase casually.
Show factory-boy mention as optional ergonomics.
Emphasize speed of keeping DB tests focused.

prompts.label: Settings split DEV and PROD
prompts.text:
Pattern a settings package: base.py, dev.py, prod.py.
Demonstrate getenv with defaults carefully (no secrets in git).
Explain DEBUG flag implications.
Mention ALLOWED_HOSTS gotcha.
List static files collectors difference briefly.
